MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number PTR-09-292356.

Appearances: Victoria Tran Sood for Adela Najarro. Richard Canatella for and with Lillian Najarro (Petitioner).

The Court stated that Mr. Canatella had filed an objection and that it was liening towards a ruling that William and Donald are beneficiaries and they have not violated the no contest clause. Ms. Tran Sood stated that this matter was set for status and that she thought there was to be an evidentiary hearing on the will contest. Mr. Canatella stated that this Court heard a matter last week and that the no contest clause doesn't reach a settlor. Ms. Tran Sood argued that the only issue on last week was the Anti-Slapp issue. The Court stated that it was prepared to go forward. Ms. Tran Sood stated that she should be able to call witnesses and that she wasn't noticed that this would be argued today. The Court stated that Adela Najarro's petition to probate the will is to be set for an evidentiary hearing and that Lillian Najarro's petitions for will contest and spousal property would trail. Ms. Tran Sood stated that all the petitions should be tried together, and informed the Court that Mr. Canatella had just filed an appeal. The matters were taken under submission.

Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgement, Or, Summary Adjudication Of Issues; Separate Statement Of Undisputed Facts; Points And Authorities; Declaration Of L. Najarro With Exhibits; Request For Judicial Notice; Proof Of Service.
Off Calendar Jul-16-2009 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Canatella for and with Lillian Najarro (Contestant). Victoria Tran Sood for Adela Najarro.

The Court read the tentative. The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative as follows:
Tentative ruling on Motion for Summary Judgment: Denied. There is a triable issue of material fact as to whether the Oneida property is in the Najarro Family Trust. Exhibits A, B and C to the declaration of Lillian Najarro do not prove as a matter of law that the Oneida property is in the Najarro Family Trust.

Tentative ruling on Motion for Summary Adjudication. Denied. Petitioner has failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b).

Evidentiary rulings.

The following objections are sustained: 4,5,6,7,8,9,12,15,16,17,18,19,20,21,and 22.

The following objections are overruled: 1,2,3,10,13 and 14.

Objection 11 is sustained on conclusionary grounds as to the term "community property" and is overruled as to the rest of the statement on both grounds raised.

Ms. Tran Sood is to prepare the order with code compliance with regard to the Motion for Summary Judgment and circulate it to Mr. Canatella.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Carl Sauceda, CSR#2031. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

Appearances: Richard Canatella for and with Lillian Najarro. Victoria Sood for Adella Najarro. Michelle McIsaac Esq. on behalf of Hartford Life. Michael Valencia for Donald Dowling.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Canatella for with Lillian Najarro. Victoria Sood for Adella Najarro (Objector). Ms. Pastora (Spouse on the property petition) was present also.

The Court confirmed that the November 3, 2008 filing is the amendment to the supplement. Mr. Canatella stated that he had meant for it to be a supplement, stated that the matter has settled as to Ms. Pastora, that the Oneida property with Ms. Sood's client is pending, that the Oneida property is community property, that Ms. Najarro as surviving spouse holds her share of the property, that discovery is being done, that the other property is listed as trust property, and that Ms. Sood's client has no standing, and that the trust property is in the spousal petition. Ms. Sood stated that she received the verified second amended supplement noting that her office does not accept service by fax, that she will waive proper service today, requested all future service by personal service or mail, that she has subpoened documents but counsel declined, that she can't move forward, and requested a ninety day continuance to bring a motion to compel. The Court questioned Mr. Canatella about the service of process regarding the will contest (1998 will). Mr. Canatella stated that Danilo Najarro was served in his office and then offered to serve others. Additional discussion regarding the service was held as set forth on the record. The Court stated there was no plausable explanation and ordered Mr. Canatella to file amended proofs of service. Mr. Canatella informed the Court that Juana Epple was avoiding service; the Court stated he would need a proof of service on the summons for Ms. Epple. Status on the amended proofs of service and service on Ms. Epple is set for January 26, 2009. The Spousal Petition is continued to March 16, 2009.
